era_context: Mid-2nd century BCE: Emperor Jing of Han (r. 157–141 BCE) is in his second year of reign, consolidating power and managing the Han state. Rome continues its hegemonic expansion in the Mediterranean. The Seleucid Empire is fragmenting under Demetrius I Soter. In India, regional kingdoms compete for dominance. This is a period of relative stability in the Han core, with ongoing frontier management and internal consolidation.

events.title: Han Dynasty: Early reign of Emperor Jing; administrative consolidation
events.description: Emperor Jing continues his consolidation of imperial authority in his second year of reign. The Han court focuses on strengthening the central bureaucracy, reducing the power of regional kingdoms, and managing frontier relations with the Xiongnu through the 'heqin' policy. No major military campaigns or political upheavals are recorded for 156 BCE specifically, but the year represents the continuation of the stable administrative order that will define Jing's reign.
events.certainty_note: The general political context is secure; no specific event is recorded for 156 BCE. The year is assigned to the broader pattern of Jing's reign.

events.title: Seleucid Empire: Demetrius I Soter's continued rule and fragmentation
events.description: Demetrius I Soter (r. 162–150 BCE) continues to rule the fragmenting Seleucid Empire. By 156 BCE, the empire is losing control of peripheral territories and facing internal opposition. No specific military or political event is recorded for 156 BCE, but the year represents the continuation of Seleucid decline and fragmentation.
events.certainty_note: The reign of Demetrius I is well-attested; the specific significance of 156 BCE is inferred from the broader trajectory of Seleucid decline.

disconfirming_evidence: No disconfirming evidence identified for events listed. The year 156 BCE is not marked by any major recorded event in surviving sources.
historiographic_note: The historical record for 156 BCE is sparse. Chinese sources provide information about Han affairs, though no specific event is recorded for this year. Mediterranean sources provide information about the Seleucid Empire and Roman hegemony, but again, no specific event is recorded for 156 BCE. The year is largely invisible in the historical record.

graph_edges.note: Continued consolidation under Jing enables Emperor Wu's expansionist policies
